prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_prescription_intake inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1)
(provisionally_assign_dispensing_site inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 dispensing_site_a)
(select_formulary_product_candidate inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(finalize_product_candidate_selection inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(route_prescription_for_authorization inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 authorization_approver_clinician_1)
(reserve_primary_inventory_lot inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 inventory_lot_primary_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(confirm_pharmacist_allocation inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 inventory_lot_primary_1 authorization_approver_clinician_1)
(release_formulary_product_candidate inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(release_authorization_approver inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 authorization_approver_clinician_1)
(register_prescription_intake outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1)
(provisionally_assign_dispensing_site outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 dispensing_site_b)
(select_formulary_product_candidate outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(finalize_product_candidate_selection outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(route_prescription_for_authorization outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 authorization_approver_clinician_1)
(reserve_secondary_inventory_lot_for_technician outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 inventory_lot_secondary_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(confirm_technician_allocation outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 inventory_lot_secondary_1 authorization_approver_clinician_1)
(release_formulary_product_candidate outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(release_authorization_approver outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 authorization_approver_clinician_1)
(create_dispense_package_standard inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 inventory_lot_primary_1 inventory_lot_secondary_1 dispense_package_1)
(select_formulary_product_candidate inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(finalize_dispense_package dispense_package_1 inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(mark_pharmacist_assignment_ready_for_pickup inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 dispense_package_1)
(mark_technician_assignment_ready_for_pickup outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 dispense_package_1)
(schedule_prescription_pickup_slot inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 patient_pickup_slot_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(finalize_substitution_and_confirm_site_allocation_pharmacist inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 dispensing_site_a patient_pickup_slot_1)
(release_formulary_product_candidate inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(select_formulary_product_candidate outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(schedule_prescription_pickup_slot outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 patient_pickup_slot_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(finalize_substitution_and_confirm_site_allocation_technician outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 dispensing_site_b patient_pickup_slot_1)
(release_formulary_product_candidate outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(register_prescription_intake dispensing_task_alpha)
(provisionally_assign_dispensing_site dispensing_task_alpha dispensing_site_a)
(select_formulary_product_candidate dispensing_task_alpha formulary_candidate_1)
(finalize_product_candidate_selection dispensing_task_alpha formulary_candidate_1)
(assign_product_component_to_task dispensing_task_alpha product_component_1)
(assign_clinical_verification_to_task dispensing_task_alpha clinical_verification_flag_1)
(route_prescription_for_authorization dispensing_task_alpha authorization_approver_clinician_1)
(reserve_and_attach_packaging_component dispensing_task_alpha packaging_component_label_1 dispense_package_1)
(stage_packaging_components_for_task dispensing_task_alpha packaging_component_label_1 dispense_package_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(assemble_components_and_mark_components_attached dispensing_task_alpha product_component_1 authorization_approver_clinician_1 packaging_component_label_1 dispense_package_1)
(perform_clinical_verification_a dispensing_task_alpha clinical_verification_flag_1 packaging_component_label_1 dispense_package_1)
(finalize_dispensing_task_without_additional_labeling dispensing_task_alpha)
(release_authorization_approver dispensing_task_alpha authorization_approver_clinician_1)
(release_product_component_from_task dispensing_task_alpha product_component_1)
(release_clinical_verification_from_task dispensing_task_alpha clinical_verification_flag_1)
(schedule_prescription_pickup_slot dispensing_task_alpha patient_pickup_slot_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(finalize_substitution_and_confirm_site_allocation_task dispensing_task_alpha dispensing_site_a patient_pickup_slot_1)
(release_formulary_product_candidate dispensing_task_alpha formulary_candidate_1)
(register_prescription_intake dispensing_task_beta)
(provisionally_assign_dispensing_site dispensing_task_beta dispensing_site_b)
(select_formulary_product_candidate dispensing_task_beta formulary_candidate_1)
(finalize_product_candidate_selection dispensing_task_beta formulary_candidate_1)
(assign_product_component_to_task dispensing_task_beta product_component_1)
(assign_clinical_verification_to_task dispensing_task_beta clinical_verification_flag_1)
(route_prescription_for_authorization dispensing_task_beta authorization_approver_clinician_1)
(reserve_and_attach_packaging_component dispensing_task_beta packaging_component_leaflet_1 dispense_package_1)
(stage_packaging_components_for_task dispensing_task_beta packaging_component_leaflet_1 dispense_package_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(assemble_components_and_mark_components_attached dispensing_task_beta product_component_1 authorization_approver_clinician_1 packaging_component_leaflet_1 dispense_package_1)
(perform_clinical_verification_a dispensing_task_beta clinical_verification_flag_1 packaging_component_leaflet_1 dispense_package_1)
(finalize_dispensing_task_without_additional_labeling dispensing_task_beta)
(release_authorization_approver dispensing_task_beta authorization_approver_clinician_1)
(release_product_component_from_task dispensing_task_beta product_component_1)
(release_clinical_verification_from_task dispensing_task_beta clinical_verification_flag_1)
(schedule_prescription_pickup_slot dispensing_task_beta patient_pickup_slot_1 formulary_candidate_1)
(finalize_substitution_and_confirm_site_allocation_task dispensing_task_beta dispensing_site_b patient_pickup_slot_1)
```

## Explanation of the plan

The provided plan systematically achieves the goal by processing each entity (inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1, outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1, dispensing_task_alpha, and dispensing_task_beta) in sequence:

1. **Assignments (pharmacy and technician)**: Registers each, assigns a dispensing site, selects and finalizes the formulary product candidate (releasing resources between steps to enable reuse), routes and confirms authorization, reserves inventory lots, creates and finalizes the shared dispense package, marks ready for pickup, schedules pickup, and confirms fulfillment—releasing sites, slots, and other resources.

2. **Tasks (alpha and beta)**: Similarly registers, assigns sites, selects/finalizes candidate, assigns shared resources (product_component, clinical_verification_flag—released between tasks), attaches and stages packaging components (using pre-linked ones), assembles, performs clinical verification (path A, as package has no auxiliary/leaflet requirements), finalizes without additional labeling, schedules pickup, and confirms fulfillment.

This ensures all predicates for fulfillment_confirmed are met without conflicts, leveraging resource releases and the domain's sequential reuse capabilities.
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex, but I need to find a plan to achieve the goals: fulfillment_confirmed for inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1, outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1, dispensing_task_alpha, and dispensing_task_beta.


## Planning task fulfillment  
- The goal is to confirm fulfillment for inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1, outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1, dispensing_task_alpha, and dispensing_task_beta.  
- Need to identify a strategy to address each task efficiently, given the complexity of the problem.

## Planning task fulfillment  
- All tasks (inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1, outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1, dispensing_task_alpha, dispensing_task_beta) are subtypes of discharge_prescription, so they can use related predicates.  
- Initially, no tasks have ready_for_fulfillment set, requiring registration via register_prescription_intake to proceed.  

## Checking initial state  
- Tasks like reserve_primary_inventory_lot need ready_for_fulfillment, but it's not set initially for any task.  
- Some resources, like formulary_candidate_1 and dispense_package_1, are shared across tasks, complicating individual processing.  

## Exploring task dependencies  
- Both dispensing_task_alpha and dispensing_task_beta are linked to the same package and assignments, suggesting a need for coordinated handling.  
- To achieve fulfillment_confirmed, tasks need pickup_scheduled, allocated_to_site, and linked_to_pickup_slot, requiring prior steps like selecting a product candidate.

## Analyzing task constraints  
- All tasks (inpatient_pharmacist_assignment_1, outpatient_pharmacy_technician_assignment_1, dispensing_task_alpha, dispensing_task_beta) are subtypes of discharge_prescription, sharing resources like formulary_candidate_1 and patient_pickup_slot_1.  
- Special handling, clinical verification flags, and product components are limited, complicating simultaneous processing.  

## Mapping fulfillment sequence  
- To achieve ready_for_fulfillment, start with register_prescription_intake, followed by provisionally_assign_dispensing_site and select_formulary_product_candidate.  
- Finalize_product_candidate_selection sets ready_for_fulfillment, enabling steps like reserve_primary_inventory_lot for inventory confirmation.  

## Exploring authorization process  
- After reserving inventory, route_prescription_for_authorization assigns an approver, followed by confirm_pharmacist_allocation to set allocation_confirmed.  
- The process requires ready_for_fulfillment and linked resources, but shared candidate availability may block parallel processing for technician tasks.

## Evaluating candidate release impacts  
- Releasing a formulary candidate after pharmacist allocation keeps ready_for_fulfillment and allocation confirmed intact.  
- This allows the candidate to become available again for technician tasks, enabling parallel processing.  

## Examining authorization dependencies  
- Authorization must be released from pharmacist tasks before routing for technician tasks, ensuring availability.  
- Releasing authorization after pharmacist confirmation enables subsequent technician routing and allocation.  

## Exploring technician allocation options  
- Technician allocation can use reserve_secondary_inventory_lot, requiring the candidate to be selected, similar to pharmacist steps.  
- Alternatively, reserve_substitution_for_technician doesn’t require selection, offering flexibility for technician processing without blocking pharmacist finalize steps.  

## Analyzing package creation and finalization  
- Creating a dispense package requires confirmed allocations and linked lots, but not candidate selection, simplifying initial setup.  
- Finalizing the package needs the pharmacist’s candidate selected, creating a dependency that may conflict if the candidate was released for technician use.  

## Considering process adjustments  
- Releasing the candidate after pharmacist allocation allows technician selection, but risks issues during package finalization.  
- Using substitution paths for technicians could resolve selection conflicts, ensuring both allocations and finalization can proceed.

## Handling candidate selection

- Releasing the formulary candidate from technician tasks makes it available for pharmacist selection, resolving conflicts during package finalization.
- Selecting the candidate for the pharmacist allows finalizing the dispense package, ensuring all allocation requirements are met.

## Managing pickup readiness

- Marking assignments ready for pickup sets the stage for scheduling, requiring the candidate to be selected for the respective role (pharmacist or technician).
- Scheduling prescription pickup slots needs a ready-for-pickup status and an available slot, linking the process to candidate availability.

## Finalizing fulfillment processes

- Finalizing substitution and confirming site allocation for both pharmacist and technician tasks requires a scheduled pickup and linked slot, marking tasks as confirmed.
- Releasing and reselecting the candidate for different roles ensures flexibility, allowing both pharmacist and technician assignments to be fulfilled.

## Evaluating Clinical Verification Needs  
- Clinical verification is required for special handling tasks, but it’s linked to a flag that must be available; without it, tasks like beta can’t proceed.  
- Releasing clinical verification from a task, like alpha, is possible after finalization, as fulfillment doesn’t depend on the link being maintained.  

## Exploring Alternative Paths for Beta  
- Beta’s hazardous flag isn’t available initially, blocking special handling; other paths to ready-for-pickup, like finalize_dispensing_task_without_additional_labeling, don’t require it.  
- Tasks can reach ready-for-pickup through verification and assembly steps, such as perform_clinical_verification, if components are assembled and linked correctly.  

## Assessing Task Assembly Requirements  
- Task components must be assembled and linked to products, packaging, and authorizations to complete verification, enabling paths to ready-for-pickup.  
- Different verification processes (a/b/c/d) depend on package requirements, like auxiliary items or leaflets, affecting how tasks move forward.

## Tracking Task Operations

- Dispensing tasks like alpha and beta require clinical verification and authorization, setting flags as not available after use.
- Operations such as assembling components and performing verification rely on staged packaging and linked resources, ensuring tasks progress.

## Finalizing Dispensing Without Additional Labeling

- Tasks can reach ready-for-pickup via finalize_dispensing_task_without_additional_labeling, requiring verification but no labeling, like alpha and beta.
- This path works without auxiliary labels or leaflets if the package doesn’t require them, streamlining the process.

## Managing Resource Releases

- Resources like product components, clinical flags, and authorizations are released sequentially after use, with packaging components staying attached.
- Different packaging components (label_1 for alpha, leaflet_1 for beta) prevent conflicts, allowing smooth task progression.

## Reviewing Task Finalization Steps  
- Task finalization requires verification completion and assembly verification, but without additional labeling, it skips patient leaflets or auxiliary items.  
- The finalize_dispensing_task_without_additional_labeling path works for packages not needing leaflets or aux labels, streamlining to ready-for-pickup.  

## Listing Task Sequence  
- The process starts with pharmacist assignment, including registering prescription intake and selecting formulary candidates.  
- Pharmacist steps include routing for authorization, reserving inventory, and confirming allocation before releasing resources.  

## Processing Technician Steps  
- Technician tasks mirror pharmacist steps, including registering intake, assigning sites, and confirming allocations with secondary inventory.  
- Resources like candidates and authorizations are released after technician confirmation, ensuring task progression.  

## Creating and Finalizing Packages  
- Packages are created by linking pharmacist and technician assignments with inventory lots, requiring selected formulary candidates.  
- Finalizing packages involves marking them ready for pickup, completing the dispensing process.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
